query.title: Self-Correcting Tool Use in LLM Agents via Execution Feedback
query.abstract: Large language model agents frequently fail multi-step tasks because erroneous tool calls go undetected until the task has already derailed. We present a framework in which agents inspect execution feedback after every tool call and revise their plan in context, without additional training. Across three interactive benchmarks our approach reduces unrecovered tool errors by 41% and improves end-to-end task success over strong reflection baselines.

candidate_pool.title: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models
candidate_pool.abstract: While large language models exhibit strong reasoning, interleaving reasoning traces with task-specific actions enables more reliable decision making in interactive environments.

candidate_pool.title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
candidate_pool.abstract: Language agents verbally reflect on task feedback signals and store reflections in episodic memory to improve decision making in subsequent trials.

candidate_pool.title: Toolformer: Language Models Can Teach Themselves to Use Tools
candidate_pool.abstract: Language models can teach themselves to use external tools via simple APIs using self-supervised tool-call annotations.

candidate_pool.title: HuggingGPT: Solving AI Tasks with ChatGPT and its Friends in Hugging Face
candidate_pool.abstract: An LLM acts as a controller that decomposes user requests and orchestrates expert models from the Hugging Face hub to solve multimodal tasks.

candidate_pool.title: Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models
candidate_pool.abstract: An open-ended embodied agent in Minecraft that continually acquires skills through an automatic curriculum and an ever-growing skill library.

candidate_pool.title: Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior
candidate_pool.abstract: Computational agents simulate believable human behavior by storing, synthesizing, and retrieving memories to plan day-to-day actions.

candidate_pool.title: Tree of Thoughts: Deliberate Problem Solving with Large Language Models
candidate_pool.abstract: Generalizes chain-of-thought prompting to deliberate problem solving with exploration and self-evaluation over coherent intermediate thoughts.

candidate_pool.title: CAMEL: Communicative Agents for "Mind" Exploration of Large Language Model Society
candidate_pool.abstract: A role-playing framework in which communicative agents cooperate autonomously to solve tasks, enabling study of multi-agent cooperation at scale.

candidate_pool.title: Self-Refine: Iterative Refinement with Self-Feedback
candidate_pool.abstract: A language model improves its initial outputs through iterative feedback and refinement generated by the same model, without supervised training.

candidate_pool.title: WebArena: A Realistic Web Environment for Building Autonomous Agents
candidate_pool.abstract: A realistic, reproducible web environment with fully functional websites for building and evaluating autonomous agents on long-horizon web tasks.

candidate_pool.title: AgentBench: Evaluating LLMs as Agents
candidate_pool.abstract: A multi-dimensional benchmark that evaluates large language models as agents across eight distinct interactive environments.

candidate_pool.title: MetaGPT: Meta Programming for a Multi-Agent Collaborative Framework
candidate_pool.abstract: A meta-programming framework encoding standardized operating procedures into multi-agent collaboration pipelines for software engineering.

candidate_pool.title: AutoGen: Enabling Next-Gen LLM Applications via Multi-Agent Conversation
candidate_pool.abstract: An open-source framework for building applications with multiple conversable agents that integrate humans, tools, and large language models.

candidate_pool.title: ToolLLM: Facilitating Large Language Models to Master 16000+ Real-world APIs
candidate_pool.abstract: A general tool-use framework with an instruction-tuning dataset covering over 16000 real-world APIs and a depth-first search based decision strategy.

candidate_pool.title: Gorilla: Large Language Model Connected with Massive APIs
candidate_pool.abstract: A finetuned model that surpasses larger LLMs on writing correct API calls and mitigates hallucination through retrieval-aware training.

candidate_pool.title: SWE-bench: Can Language Models Resolve Real-World GitHub Issues?
candidate_pool.abstract: A benchmark evaluating whether language models can resolve real GitHub issues by generating patches that pass repository test suites.

candidate_pool.title: MemGPT: Towards LLMs as Operating Systems
candidate_pool.abstract: An OS-inspired system that virtualizes LLM context through memory tiers and interrupt-driven control flow, enabling unbounded conversations.

candidate_pool.title: Language Agent Tree Search Unifies Reasoning, Acting, and Planning in Language Models
candidate_pool.abstract: Adapts Monte Carlo tree search to language agents, unifying reasoning, acting, and planning with environment feedback and self-reflection.

candidate_pool.title: FireAct: Toward Language Agent Fine-tuning
candidate_pool.abstract: Fine-tuning language models with agent trajectories collected from multiple tasks and prompting methods consistently improves agent capabilities.

candidate_pool.title: AgentTuning: Enabling Generalized Agent Abilities for LLMs
candidate_pool.abstract: Instruction-tuning with interaction trajectories enhances generalized agent abilities of LLMs while preserving their general capabilities.

candidate_pool.title: GAIA: A Benchmark for General AI Assistants
candidate_pool.abstract: Real-world assistant questions requiring reasoning, multi-modality handling, web browsing, and tool use; conceptually easy for humans yet hard for AIs.

candidate_pool.title: CRITIC: Large Language Models Can Self-Correct with Tool-Interactive Critiquing
candidate_pool.abstract: Enables LLMs to validate and progressively amend their own outputs through interaction with external tools, similar to human verification behavior.

candidate_pool.title: ChatDev: Communicative Agents for Software Development
candidate_pool.abstract: A virtual chat-powered software company in which specialized agents collaborate through dialogue across design, coding, and testing phases.

candidate_pool.title: Lemur: Harmonizing Natural Language and Code for Language Agents
candidate_pool.abstract: An openly accessible model balancing natural language and coding capabilities to serve as a foundation for executable language agents.

candidate_pool.title: Teaching Large Language Models to Self-Debug
candidate_pool.abstract: Teaches large language models to debug their predicted programs by explaining generated code and leveraging execution results, without human feedback.

candidate_pool.title: Cognitive Architectures for Language Agents
candidate_pool.abstract: A conceptual framework organizing language agents along modular memory, structured action space, and generalized decision-making dimensions.

candidate_pool.title: AgentVerse: Facilitating Multi-Agent Collaboration and Exploring Emergent Behaviors
candidate_pool.abstract: A multi-agent framework that dynamically adjusts group composition, enabling emergent collaborative behaviors among expert agents.

candidate_pool.title: InterCode: Standardizing and Benchmarking Interactive Coding with Execution Feedback
candidate_pool.abstract: A lightweight, extensible framework standardizing interactive coding as a reinforcement learning environment with execution feedback as rewards.

candidate_pool.title: ToolAlpaca: Generalized Tool Learning for Language Models with 3000 Simulated Cases
candidate_pool.abstract: Demonstrates generalized tool-use in compact language models via automatically generated multi-turn tool-use simulations across diverse tools.

candidate_pool.title: SWE-agent: Agent-Computer Interfaces Enable Automated Software Engineering
candidate_pool.abstract: A custom agent-computer interface enables language model agents to autonomously navigate repositories, edit code, and run tests to fix real issues.

gold_related_work.raw: Early agent frameworks interleave reasoning and acting, grounding model decisions in environment observations \cite{yao2022react}. A complementary line of work improves agent behavior through self-generated feedback: Reflexion converts episodic failures into verbal reinforcement \cite{shinn2023reflexion}, Self-Refine iteratively revises outputs using the model's own critiques \cite{madaan2023selfrefine}, and CRITIC validates and amends generations through tool-interactive critiquing \cite{gou2023critic}. Closest to our setting, self-debugging teaches models to repair generated programs from execution results \cite{chen2023selfdebug}, and InterCode formalizes interactive coding with execution feedback as a reinforcement learning environment \cite{yang2023intercode}. On the tool-use side, ToolLLM scales instruction tuning to thousands of real-world APIs but corrects errors only by re-planning \cite{qin2023toolllm}. In contrast, we close the loop between tool execution feedback and in-context self-correction without any additional training.
gold_related_work.id_mapped: Early agent frameworks interleave reasoning and acting, grounding model decisions in environment observations [P01]. A complementary line of work improves agent behavior through self-generated feedback: Reflexion converts episodic failures into verbal reinforcement [P02], Self-Refine iteratively revises outputs using the model's own critiques [P09], and CRITIC validates and amends generations through tool-interactive critiquing [P22]. Closest to our setting, self-debugging teaches models to repair generated programs from execution results [P25], and InterCode formalizes interactive coding with execution feedback as a reinforcement learning environment [P28]. On the tool-use side, ToolLLM scales instruction tuning to thousands of real-world APIs but corrects errors only by re-planning [P14]. In contrast, we close the loop between tool execution feedback and in-context self-correction without any additional training.
